patch.target: Drop the editorial byline and write in first person throughout, referencing the author's actual trip with named friends.
patch.before: Drop the editorial byline and write in first person throughout, referencing the author's actual trip with named friends.

instructions.evidence: Genuine opinions vs safe claims 6/10 — Has some opinions (skip culture day one) but many FAQ answers are bland and hedged.

patch.target: Oahu is set up for both, beaches, hikes, and museums are family-proof, but most friend groups will want to set expectations for late dinners and city nightlife if traveling with kids.
patch.before: Oahu is set up for both, beaches, hikes, and museums are family-proof, but most friend groups will want to set expectations for late dinners and city nightlife if traveling with kids.
patch.after: Oahu works for both, but mixing toddlers with a friend group that wants Bar 35 at midnight is a recipe for resentment—pick one mode.
patch.rationale: Humanization axis "Genuine opinions vs safe claims" scored 6/10: Has some opinions (skip culture day one) but many FAQ answers are bland and hedged.

instructions.evidence: Unexpected phrasings 6/10 — Some fresh lines ('one organiser, seven opinions, 1000 unread messages') but mixed with generic travel-blog filler.

patch.target: Coordinating friends in Oahu requires more than wishful thinking, it needs a plan with real places and actual decision points, or you'll end up missing sunset at Pipeline, again.
patch.before: Coordinating friends in Oahu requires more than wishful thinking, it needs a plan with real places and actual decision points, or you'll end up missing sunset at Pipeline, again.
patch.after: Coordinating friends in Oahu takes more than wishful thinking—without real places and decision points, you'll watch Pipeline's sunset through the Costco parking lot, again.
patch.rationale: Humanization axis "Unexpected phrasings" scored 6/10: Some fresh lines ('one organiser, seven opinions, 1000 unread messages') but mixed with generic travel-blog filler.

instructions.evidence: Quotable sentences 5/10 — A few quotable moments but many ruined by comma splices and awkward phrasing.

patch.target: Friend groups don't need more platitudes, you need dates, places, and backup backups.
patch.before: Friend groups don't need more platitudes, you need dates, places, and backup backups.
patch.after: Friend groups don't need platitudes—they need dates, confirmation numbers, and a designated grown-up who'll book Hanauma Bay while everyone else debates flights.
patch.rationale: Humanization axis "Quotable sentences" scored 5/10: A few quotable moments but many ruined by comma splices and awkward phrasing.

instructions.evidence: intro_hook 6/10 — The post buries its best sentence ('There's a specific moment on every group trip when someone suggests Hanauma Bay...') several paragraphs down after a generic two-sentence open that restates the title. The TL;DR and Quick Answer blocks also clutter the opening before the reader gets any narrative pull. The Hanauma Bay moment is vivid and earned; it should be sentence one.

patch.target: Coordinating an Oahu group trip shouldn't be chaos. Here's a day-by-day, friend-proof 7-day itinerary using real spots, plus what actually matters for planning.
patch.before: Coordinating an Oahu group trip shouldn't be chaos. Here's a day-by-day, friend-proof 7-day itinerary using real spots, plus what actually matters for planning.
patch.after: There's a specific moment on every Oahu group trip when someone suggests Hanauma Bay and you realize nobody booked it—and now it's closed Monday, it's Tuesday, and you've lost a day. This itinerary exists to kill that moment before it kills your trip.
patch.rationale: Quality axis "intro_hook" scored 6/10: The post buries its best sentence ('There's a specific moment on every group trip when someone suggests Hanauma Bay...') several paragraphs down after a generic two-sentence open that restates the title. The TL;DR and Quick Answer blocks also clutter the opening before the reader gets any narrative pull. The Hanauma Bay moment is vivid and earned; it should be sentence one.
